domain:
In the context of a theater organization, various plays are performed in one or more theaters, each identified by its own location, for example "The Northwest Theater". A play may have multiple scheduled sessions, and each session occurs on a scheduled date. Plays are identified by a unique name and is either a musical or an opera. Furthermore, each play may take place during the day or at night.

Each play is written by one or more authors, who are uniquely identified by name. Plays involve participants each also identified by name, and the same participant may act in multiple plays. For each play session, the starting time and an ending time is recorded, and participants can act in one or more sessions. Both authors and participant can be the same person, who may write a play and participate in its performance.

questions.question:
In the context of a author, is it always associated with at least one play, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. An author can exist without any play.
2. An author must have at least one play.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: An author can exist without any play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: An author must have at least one play.
questions.selected: Option 1: An author can exist without any play.

questions.question:
In the context of an author, can there be more than one play involved, or is it always associated with just one? 
For example:
1. An author is linked to a single play.
2. An author is linked to multiple plays.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Each author is always linked to a single play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: An author can be linked to multiple plays.
questions.selected: Option 2: An author can be linked to multiple plays.

questions.option_1: Option 1: A participant can exist without any author.

questions.selected: Option 1: A participant can exist without any author.

questions.question:
In the context of a participant, can there be more than one author involved, or is it always associated with just one? 
For example:
1. A participant is linked to a single author.
2. A participant is linked to multiple authors.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Each participant is always linked to a single author.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A participant can be linked to multiple authors.
questions.selected: Option 2: A participant can be linked to multiple authors.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 2: A participant can be linked to multiple authors.

This option is better because the domain description states that plays are written by one or more authors, and it also mentions that the same person can be both an author and a participant, implying that a participant can be associated with multiple authors.

questions.question:
We know that each theater has a name. When we think about name, is it just a simple piece of information, like a line of text? 
Or is name made up of different parts that might be important?
For example:
1. For each name, the detail information is saved separately, so users can filter theater by detail.
2. The name is a single piece of text. This works well when we just need to show the name.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: We need the name details separately.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A single line of text for name is enough.
questions.selected: Option 2: A single line of text for name is enough.

questions.question:
When thinking about the name of theaters, are there only specific, predefined values it can have, or can it be any text?
For example:
1. The name can be any text value that users want to enter.
2. The name must be one of these specific values: northwest theater, downtown theater, broadway theater or community theater.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The name can be any text value.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The name must be one of these: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: The name can be any text value.

questions.question:
Does a session always require a theater to exist. 
If a theater is removed, would all the associated sessions also need to be removed?

For example:
1. A session belongs to a theater and cannot exist on its own.
2. A session is linked to a theater but can exist independently of it.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: A session always belongs to a theater.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A session is linked to theater but can exist independently.
questions.selected: Option 1: A session always belongs to a theater.

questions.question:
In the context of a session, is it always associated with at least one theater, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. A session can exist without any theater.
2. A session must have at least one theater.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: A session can exist without any theater.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A session must have at least one theater.
questions.selected: Option 2: A session must have at least one theater.

questions.question:
The concept session potentially represents a link between theaters and plays. How should we understand this connection?

For example:
1. A session is a concept, but it does not represent a link between a theater and a play.
2. A session is a concept and a link that connects a theater and a play. 

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The session does not represent a link between a theater and a play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The session is a concept and a link between a theater and a play.
questions.selected: Option 2: The session is a concept and a link between a theater and a play.
